text:
SAINT QUENTIN-EN-YVELINES, France 芒聙聰 Three former U.S. Open champions are in the field for the men芒聙聶s Olympic Golf Tournament. But not the most recent one.

In fact, Bryson DeChambeau has won two of the past five U.S. Opens and芒聙聰although he would have played in Japan in 2021 but was forced out due to COVID-19芒聙聰he芒聙聶s not at Le Golf Nationalfor the start of the tournament Thursday.

Wyndham Clark is and it芒聙聶s easy to suggest that DeChambeau is more deserving, certainly based on recency bias.

DeChambeau finished in the top six in three major championships this year; Clark missed the cut in three. In the one major where Clark made the cut芒聙聰the U.S. Open芒聙聰DeChambeau was the winner.

It is under that backdrop that Clark arrived outside of Paris looking to get back to the form he showed earlier this year when he won at Pebble Beach and contended at both the Arnold Palmer Invitational and Players Championship芒聙聰finishing second at both to Scottie Scheffler.

芒聙聹It芒聙聶s been a unique year,芒聙聺 Clark said Wednesday in advance of the tournament. 芒聙聹I started out fantastic and in previous years on Tour, I typically have kind of played better at the end or the year.

芒聙聹But I kind of have some injuries I've been dealing with, happened right before Houston leading into the Masters, and I haven't been the same. I feel like I'm finally getting to where I can swing at the level that I know I can swing, and so that's kind of been an ongoing issue and it's been frustrating for me.

芒聙聹Then I've got to be honest, I've been very frustrated with how I played at the majors. I believe that I wasn't showing how good of a player I know I can be, and especially at highest level in the majors. But I've also got to look at how far I've come in two years. I'm fifth on the FedEx in back-to-back years and I've won multiple times and I have a lot of top finishes and I'm here at the Olympics.

芒聙聹So I've got to make sure I don't let the outside people put too much pressure on myself because I've grown so much in these two years. It's not where I want to be in the future, but years like this, I've learned a lot and I'm hoping I take all the things I learned and play better in the majors next year.芒聙聺

The other U.S. Open champions in the field are Spain芒聙聶s Jon Rahm (2021) and England芒聙聶s Matt Fitzpatrick (2022). Clark won last year at Los Angeles Country Club and added the victory earlier this year at Pebble Beach.

Clark was never really in big danger on missing out on the Olympics. Despite his poor form in the majors, he had those high finishes at Bay Hill and the Players. He tied for third at the RBC Heritage. That pushed him high enough in the rankings that he was able to stay among the top four Americans through the U.S. Open.

It was Collin Morikawa who was sweating it out. The two-time major winner won in the fall at the Zozo Championship but was battling for his Olympic spot through the U.S. Open, edging Patrick Cantlay for the final position at the end of the qualifying deadline which came via the Official World Golf Ranking.

Morikawa finished tied for 14th at the U.S. Open. Cantlay needed a two-way tie for second at the U.S. Open芒聙聰basically one shot芒聙聰and finished third to finish behind him and out of the Olympics.

芒聙聹When I look at it and compare right where I am now to where I was in January and February and March, there's been a lot of big wins,芒聙聺 Morikawa said. 芒聙聹I've worked my ass off to get here and just because I made it doesn't mean it's over and doesn't mean I'm getting my paddles back.

芒聙聹I've been here before (at the 2021 Olympics), and I know what it feels like to honestly lose in a playoff for Bronze and that sucks. There's a lot of motivation just pushing yourself to come out and play some really good golf.芒聙聺

Morikawa was in the final group with Scheffler at the Masters before finishing tied for third and he tied for fourth at the Masters, playing with Schauffele in the final group

He was also tied for fourth at the Scottish Open before tying for 16th脗聽at the British Open.

Clark tied for ninth at the Travelers and tied for 10th at the Scottish with a strong final round, then missed the cut at Royal Troon.

芒聙聹Ever since the Masters, I haven't been able to get up to the speed that I normally have with driver and I've been hitting it a lot shorter,芒聙聺 Clark said. 芒聙聹It's been a lot of things where it led me down a path of, is it something in my swing, is it equipment, and then come full circle, we just realize now I'm a lot healthier, and maybe there was a couple equipment things.

芒聙聹But you start going down rabbit holes that maybe I shouldn't have gone down and next thing you know, I'm missing cuts by a little bit and not playing at the level that I was earlier in the year or some parts of last year.芒聙聺

Clark said he芒聙聶s gotten a boost playing with Scheffler as his partner in practice rounds against Schauffele and Morikawa.

He芒聙聶s also soaked up the experience, arriving for and taking part in the Opening Ceremony. He said he芒聙聶s attended at least one Olympic event per night through Tuesday, including gymnastics.

芒聙聹For me it only inspires you to see how hard they work, and they come to this moment,芒聙聺 Clark said. 芒聙聹It's amazing that they excel under the lights when they have to. I really appreciate what they do and inspired, to be honest.芒聙聺

Bob Harig is a senior writer covering golf for Sports Illustrated. He has more than 25 years experience on the beat, including 15 at ESPN. Harig is a regular guest on Sirius XM PGA Tour Radio and has written two books, 芒聙聹DRIVE: The Lasting Legacy of Tiger Woods芒聙聺 and 芒聙聹Tiger and Phil: Golf芒聙聶s Most Fascinating Rivalry.芒聙聺 He graduated from Indiana University where he earned an Evans Scholarship, named in honor of the great amateur golfer Charles (Chick) Evans Jr. Harig, a former president of the Golf Writers Association of America, lives in Clearwater, Fla.

title: After a Frustrating Majors Season, Wyndham Clark Seeks Olympic Redemption
description: The 2023 U.S. Open champion missed the cut in three of the four majors this year while battling injuries and said he's finally getting his swing back.
